mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains below recent levels, equity discount rates ease and risk appetite improves, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate declines do not coincide with stronger short-horizon index performance.
failure_condition: If triggered by declining FEDFUNDS, 20d SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of observations or the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A threshold move in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive level tightens financial conditions and raises the equity discount rate, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest immediately after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if, after FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, SPX delivers positive 20d returns in at least 55% of cases or the average 20d return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth indicates weakening household income and lower final demand, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires both labor-market deterioration and payroll softness.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS does not improve downside prediction, or if the interaction term has no incremental explanatory power over either proxy alone for 20d returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS signals stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports equity earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be most visible when sales growth is broad-based rather than isolated.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising RSAFS are followed by negative 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal’s rolling 2-year Rank IC is persistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO above its recent trend reflects improving industrial output and better near-term corporate activity, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is a short-horizon cyclical continuation effect from real activity strength.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO is not associated with positive 5d SPX returns more than 55% of the time or if the sign of the effect flips in a rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST drops sharply while RSAFS remains firm, housing weakness is not offset by consumer demand and the market should reprice toward slower growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint pattern is stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined condition of falling HOUST and steady RSAFS does not produce below-average 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction does not outperform either proxy individually.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, declining UNRATE together with rising PAYEMS signals reemployment and improving household cash flow, which should drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when labor gains are purely transitory and do not broaden into demand.
failure_condition: Reject if falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS do not predict above-median 60d SPX returns, or if the joint signal loses significance after controlling for either labor proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is expanding while CPIAUCSL is stable or falling, real growth improves without an inflation overhang, supporting equity multiples and earnings over the next 60 trading days. The signal is refuted if real growth coincides with persistent inflation acceleration.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes of rising GDPC1 with stable or declining CPIAUCSL do not outperform the unconditional 60d return distribution, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.
